key_insight: OptiLoop shows that LLM-generated optimization agents can appear correct in isolation yet still fail semantically during coordination, so they must be verified and repaired inside the decision loop itself.
engineering_relevance: This is useful for building decentralized optimization systems where agents must respect private objectives and constraints while coordinating reliably with other parties.
method: Generate local optimization agents from natural-language specs
Run short bounded ADMM-style coordination checks against a fixed reference counterparty
Use extracted behavioral/static evidence to trigger code fixes or corrected-formulation repair
evidence_signal: On 40 held-out scenarios, OptiLoop-Full raised objective match from 66.0% to 93.0% and social match from 68.5% to 89.0%, while cutting mean objective gap from 15.3% to 3.5% and social gap from 7.6% to 2.0%.
watch_item: The approach depends on a reference counterparty and bounded coordination tests, so performance may vary when real deployments have more complex dynamics or weaker observability.
paper_title: OptiLoop: Coordination-in-the-Loop Verification and Repair for LLM-Generated Optimization Agents
